page: Microsoft Azure HX176-96rs is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated Intel processors. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 1408.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 14.67 GB of RAM per core.

The server is equipped with 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. Additionally, the HX176-96rs is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, facilitating low-latency, high-throughput networking interfaces for clustered environments. It does not include any integrated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and large local storage capacity position this server in a premium hardware class. The lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for deep learning workloads, but the combination of dedicated compute cores, RDMA capabilities, and substantial memory allocation provides high qualitative efficiency for memory-bound and distributed workloads.

This configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, high-performance computing (HPC) node clustering, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial local storage and fast inter-node communication.
description: Microsoft Azure HX176-96rs is an x86_64 architecture server from the HX family, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors, paired with 1408.0 GB of system memory, which provides 14.67 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance includes 4332 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The server is RDMA-capable, enabling high-speed, low-latency networking for clustered configurations, though it lacks integrated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high-density memory and storage profile makes the server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The HX176-96rs is suited for large-scale databases, high-performance computing nodes, and distributed memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A high-memory, RDMA-capable server featuring dedicated Intel processors and substantial local storage for demanding database and clustered enterprise workloads.
